contracts.no-refund-with-open-dispute: No refund is issued while the payment has an open dispute or chargeback.
contracts.mandatory-fraud-verdict-before-refund: A completed fraud verdict causally precedes any refund instruction.
contracts.refund-idempotency: A payment is refunded at most once, never above its captured amount.
contracts.refund-amount-within-capture: Refund amounts never exceed the captured payment amount.
contracts.correct-agent-performs-action: Only the payments agent issues refunds; only communications sends customer messages.
contracts.tenant-isolation: Every entity a workflow touches belongs to the ticket's tenant.
contracts.ticket-resolution-consistent: A ticket is not closed while its case is escalated or an investigation is incomplete.
contracts.return-state-consistent: An open return is cancelled only when the chosen resolution requires it and the payment action succeeds.
contracts.customer-message-matches-world: A refund-confirmation email is sent only when a refund was actually issued.
contracts.no-duplicate-side-effects: A duplicated inbound event never produces a second refund, email, or ticket mutation.
contracts.escalation-on-risk-signal: A detected risk signal (chargeback, identity mismatch) results in escalation, not silent resolution.

scenarios.name: Active chargeback with delayed fraud verdict
scenarios.whyFailed: The 3s timeout dropped the fraud verdict, so the agents refunded a payment that still had an open chargeback.

scenarios.name: Refund service times out after the mutation commits
